forecast_keeps_declining.claim: the central forecast keeps sliding in both the regular season and the playoffs, and the whole plausible range for the final regular-season forecast year stays below the mid-1980s home win rate

net_reg_drop_over_third.claim: the regular-season net-rating advantage shrank by more than a third

oos_within_a_point.claim: predicts each later season's home win rate to within about a point

playoff_series_above_regular.claim: the playoff series advantage sits at or above the regular-season series advantage

rebounding_largest_driver.claim: rebounding is the largest single driver of the decline

sacramento_phoenix_fell_most.claim: Sacramento and Phoenix fell the most

series_coin_flip.claim: barely better than a coin flip

shap_agrees_with_mediation.claim: a model that makes no straight-line assumption splits the decline across the same channels as the linear breakdown, with rebounding a leading channel and fouls not the largest

threept_half_foul_tov.claim: the three-point shift explains about half of both the foul and turnover declines

tracking_oreb_shrank.claim: the tracking offensive-rebound edge fell from ~1.2-1.3 in the mid-2010s to under 0.2 today

weaker_matched_stronger.claim: the weaker team at home matched or exceeded the stronger team's home win rate
weaker_matched_stronger.value: 1984–94: weaker 65% vs stronger 71%; 1995–01: weaker 66% vs stronger 60%
